content: While auditing RLS policies on NDF.CTG (a live community platform, 200+ users), I found a role-escalation vulnerability — a policy was missing its WITH CHECK clause. Without it, a member could update their own row and silently promote themselves to admin. The USING clause was doing its job on reads, but writes had no equivalent guard. Fixed it, then went back and audited all 9 tables for the same gap. It's the kind of bug that's invisible until someone exploits it — no error, no crash, just a quiet privilege escalation. Now it's part of my checklist for every new RLS policy: never assume USING covers writes.
